title: pseudocytisus integrifolius subsp. glabrescens
intro: Pseudocytisus integrifolius subsp. glabrescens, also known as Cytisus integrifolius subsp. glabrescens, is a plant species within the Fabaceae family, commonly found in the Iberian Peninsula, characterized by its woody stems and greenish-yellow flowers. Pseudocytisus integrifolius subsp. glabrescens has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the flowers and leaves of Pseudocytisus integrifolius subsp. glabrescens are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
